user_symptom: We can confirm increased delays for CloudWatch log event processing for metric filter extraction and log subscriptions in the EU-WEST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into 'INSUFFICIENT_DATA' state if set on metrics extracted using log filters.

We are investigating increased error rates with the CloudWatch GetMetricStatistics and GetMetricData APIs when requesting metrics older than 3 hours in the US-EAST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into 'INSUFFICIENT_DATA' state if the period of the alarm is longer than 3 hours.
customers experienced intermittent delays in the availability of CloudWatch metrics for load balancers in the EU-WEST-1 Region. Connectivity to load balancers was not affected.
We are investigating increased faults for CloudWatch alarms APIs and delays in processing some alarms in the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region.

We are investigating increased faults and latencies for CloudWatch Metrics PutMetricData and CloudWatch Logs PutLogEvents APIs in the AP-EAST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on delayed metrics. Some customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch Metrics PutMetricData and CloudWatch Logs PutLogEvents APIs in the AP-EAST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state, and some metric data may be missing.

We can confirm increased error rates for CloudWatch Logs Insights queries in the US-EAST-1 Region. Customers running queries using the StartQuery API may not be able to successfully schedule the query execution. Between March 3, 8:25 PM and March 4, 6:17 AM PST, some customers experienced increased delays for log events in query results in CloudWatch Logs Insights in the US-EAST-1 Region.

We are investigating increased error rates and latencies for CloudWatch Logs APIs in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region. We can confirm elevated API error rates and some delayed log events in AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region. We have identified the root cause of the elevated API error rates and some delayed log events in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region. We continue to work on investigating and resolving increased error rates and latencies for CloudWatch Logs APIs in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region. We have completed a mitigation strategy that we anticipated would resolve the increased error rates and latencies for CloudWatch Logs APIs in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region. While we have seen some recovery in error rates, we continue to see elevated levels of latency and continue working towards full resolution. We are deploying a second mitigation strategy to resolve elevated latency and the remaining level of errors for CloudWatch Logs APIs in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region. Between 7:01 AM and 4:20 PM PDT we experienced increased error rates and latencies for CloudWatch Logs APIs in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region.

We are investigating increased faults and latencies for CloudWatch APIs and metrics in the EU-WEST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on delayed metrics. Between 5:15 AM PDT and 6:31 AM PDT, some customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch APIs in the EU-WEST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

Between 4:38 PM and 5:35 PM PDT a small number of instances were unavailable due to power loss in a single Availability Zone (usw1-az1) in the US-WEST-1 Region. During this time, some customers also experienced insufficient-data in the results from EC2 instance status checks for instances that were not impacted by the power event. During this time, CloudWatch alarms may have transitioned into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on the delayed metrics.
degraded performance for some EBS volumes in the US-EAST-2 Region. During this time, some customers also experienced insufficient-data in the results from EC2 instance status checks.
customers may have experienced elevated alarms API faults and delayed alarms in the EU-WEST-2 Region.

We are investigating increased faults for CloudWatch Logs APIs in the US-WEST-2 Region. Log events and metrics may be delayed. Alarms may transition into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on delayed metrics. We can confirm increased faults for CloudWatch Logs APIs and some delayed log events in the US-WEST-2 Region. Between 5:56 PM and 10:15 PM PDT, some customers experienced increased delays for CloudWatch log events in the US-WEST-2 Region. Metrics filtered from those log events were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics may have transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

We are investigating elevated rate of API faults and delays in processing some alarms in the US-EAST-1 Region. We can confirm that scheduled events are not being delivered via CloudWatch Events in the US-EAST-1 Region. AWS Lambda functions that are invoked in response to CloudWatch events may not be getting invoked successfully. We have identified the root cause of the errors in scheduled event creation. AWS Lambda functions that are invoked in response to scheduled CloudWatch events are not getting invoked successfully. Starting at 5:27 PM PDT, there were delays delivering scheduled CloudWatch events.

We are investigating increased delays for CloudWatch metrics in the US-EAST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on delayed metrics. We continue to investigate elevated API error rates and increased delays for CloudWatch metrics in the US-EAST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on delayed metrics. Between 7:16 PM and 9:08 PM PST, some customers experienced elevated error rates when calling CloudWatch APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.
Customers experienced elevated API fault rates and latencies in the EU-WEST-2 Region. Some metrics were also delayed initially.

We are investigating increased delays for CloudWatch metrics in the EU-WEST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into 'INSUFFICIENT_DATA' state if set on delayed metrics. Some customers may have experienced intermittent delays in the availability of CloudWatch ELB metrics in the EU-WEST-1 Region.

We are investigating increased API error rates and latencies in the US-WEST-1 Region. APIs also returned insufficient-data in the results from EC2 instance status checks and CloudWatch alarms transitioned into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on the delayed metrics.

We are investigating increased delays for CloudWatch metrics in the US-WEST-2 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into 'INSUFFICIENT_DATA' state if set on delayed metrics. We can confirm increased connection errors and latencies for CloudWatch APIs. Some CloudWatch metrics are delayed.

Increased delays for CloudWatch log event processing for metric filter extraction and log subscriptions. Metrics extracted using Logs Filter Patterns were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on those delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.
increased error rates and delays for CloudWatch Logs Insights queries
customers experienced elevated errors listing metrics via the CloudWatch ListMetrics API and the CloudWatch Management Console in the US-WEST-2 Region.

Customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch Logs APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Log events and metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

We are investigating increased delays for CloudWatch metric data extracted from CloudWatch Logs in the AP-SOUTHEAST-2 Region. CloudWatch alarms dependent on metrics extracted from logs may transition into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state. Between 4:38 PM and 5:43 PM PST, some customers experienced increased delays for CloudWatch metrics extracted from CloudWatch Logs in the AP-SOUTHEAST-2 Region. CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics may have transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

We are investigating increased error rates and latencies for CloudWatch Logs APIs in the US-WEST-2 Region. Log events and metrics may be delayed. Alarms may transition into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on delayed metrics. Between 12:41 PM and 1:07 PM PST, we experienced elevated error rates when calling CloudWatch Logs APIs in the US-WEST-2 Region. Some log events and metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.
customers experienced increased faults for CloudWatch alarms APIs and delays in processing some alarms in the US-EAST-1 Region.

scheduled events are not being delivered via CloudWatch Events in the US-WEST-2 Region. AWS Lambda functions that are invoked in response to CloudWatch events may not be getting invoked successfully. Starting at 5:27 PM PDT, there were delays delivering scheduled CloudWatch events.

We are investigating increased faults and latencies for CloudWatch APIs and metrics in the EU-WEST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on delayed metrics. Between 3:13 AM and 4:37 AM PDT, some customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch APIs in the EU-WEST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

Some customers experienced elevated error rates when calling CloudWatch APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into the INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

We are investigating increased provisioning latencies for new load balancers, delayed propagation of load balancer CloudWatch metrics, and inconsistent health check statuses from the DescribeTargetHealth API for Network Load Balancers in the US-EAST-1 Region. Connectivity to existing load balancers is not affected.

Customers experienced elevated faults when making GetMetricStatistics API requests for metrics data more than two days old in the US-EAST-1 Region and CloudWatch Dashboards were unavailable. CloudWatch logs customers experienced elevated API faults and delayed log events.
We are investigating latency in the ability to search contacts, and the inability to add contacts to rooms and calls in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We are investigating increased API error rates in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.
We are investigating increased error rates and latency for the Identity, Registry, and Rules Engine APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Device connectivity, messaging and rules evaluations are unaffected.
We are investigating increased invoke and API error rates in the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region. We can confirm increased invoke and API error rates in the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region.
We continue to see increased error rates and latencies for invokes in the US-EAST-1 region.
We are investigating increased API error rates and latencies for the Amazon EC2 Container Service APIs in the US-WEST-2 Region. Container instance connectivity and running tasks are not affected.
ECS experienced increased API error rates, latencies, and task launch failures. Fargate task launches are currently experiencing increased insufficient capacity errors.
We are currently experiencing increased error rates in the GetRecommendations API in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We are investigating increased API error rates in AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.
We are currently investigating increased error rates for the AWS Management Console in the US-WEST-2 Region.
Applications not processing data
We are investigating elevated error rates for the VPC Management Console in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region. The VPC Management Console experienced elevated error rates in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region.
We are investigating increased error rates for ELB APIs in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region. Connectivity to existing load balancers is not affected.

We are investigating increased API error rates in the US-EAST-1 Region. Between 12:43 PM and 12:59 PM PST, we experienced increased error rates and latencies for some AWS services within the US-EAST-1 Region.

We are investigating increased error rates and latencies for the EC2 APIs in the US-WEST-2 Region. We can confirm increased error rates and latencies for the EC2 network-related APIs and elevated launch failures for newly launched instances within the US-WEST-2 Region.
Increased Error Rates affecting the delivery of sample applications and custom SDKs, as well as the creation of APIs in the Cloud Logic feature.

We are investigating increased API errors for API Gateway in the US-EAST-2 Region. We have confirmed that the elevated API error rate is restricted to control plane operations for API Gateway Version 2 API (WebSocket and HTTP APIs).

We are investigating errors affecting the Billing Console including the Dashboard and the Bills page. Between 12:28 PM and 2:10 PM PST we experienced increased error rates affecting the Billing Console including the Dashboard and the Bills page.
We are investigating increased error rates and latencies for ELB APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region. This issue does not affect traffic on running load balancers.

We can confirm increased deployment error rates in the US-EAST-1 Region. Between 9:37 AM PST and 5:20 PM PST we experienced an increased error rate while executing deployments, in the US-EAST-1 Region.
elevated API error rates in the EU-WEST-1 Region
We are investigating increased API error rates for the ECR APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region.
delays in starting and terminating clusters in the US-EAST-1 Region; delays in running steps, as well as starting and terminating Amazon EMR clusters
Beginning at 11:43 AM PST some Amazon Aurora clusters experienced increased database create times and cluster unavailability in the CA-CENTRAL-1 Region.
Customers may experience issues creating or updating their metric streams. Delivery of metric updates for the existing metric streams is not affected.
We are investigating increased error rates and latencies for the EC2 APIs in the US-WEST-2 Region.
increased API fault rates and latencies in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region

Kinesis Data Streams customers are still experiencing increased API errors. As a result customers are not able to write or read data published to Kinesis streams. CloudWatch metrics and events are also affected, with elevated PutMetricData API error rates and some delayed metrics. AutoScaling is also experiencing delays in scaling times due to CloudWatch metric delays. Kinesis Data Streams API operations are seeing gradual recovery but customers may continue to experience increased latencies and failure rates.
increased database create times and cluster unavailability for Amazon Aurora

We have confirmed increased error rates for binding certificates and first time certificate issuance in the US-EAST-1 Region. We have also confirmed increased error rates regarding validation emails in all regions. We also saw delays in validation e-mails in all regions.
We are investigating increased error rates affecting a small number of customers running Amazon Athena queries in the US-EAST-1 Region.

customers experienced elevated latency and error rates in response to IAM API requests, as well as delays in describing recently created or modified IAM resources. Propagation of IAM API updates was delayed, and newly created or recently updated IAM users, credentials, roles, and policies may not have been available for authentication and authorization.
S3 customers saw elevated errors and latency for a subset of API requests to create and manage Amazon S3 buckets.
Affected customers experiencing issues when attempting SSO authentication for the AWS console and services.
increased errors when calling WorkSpaces APIs and connecting to WorkSpaces in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We are investigating increased error rates for API calls in the US-EAST-1 Region.

We are investigating increased latencies for processing some alarms in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region. We continue to investigate delays in processing some alarms in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region. Between 3:03 AM and 5:42 AM PDT, customers may have experienced some delayed alarms in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region.
We are investigating increased Workmail Outlook latencies in the US-EAST-1 Region impacting the sending and receiving of new messages or appointments in Outlook clients.

We are investigating increased error rates and latencies affecting multiple services and the AWS Console in the SA-EAST-1 Region. The AWS Management Console is also experiencing elevated error rates as a result of this issue.
elevated error rates and connection failures in the US-EAST-1 Region
Between 2:50 AM and 4:55 AM PDT we experienced increased API error rates in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We are investigating increased errors when launching new WorkSpaces in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
elevated latencies and API error rates
We are investigating increased Git Push/Pull error rates via the SSH protocol in the US-EAST-2 Region.
We are investigating increased error rates for send and receive operations for FIFO Queues in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We are investigating increased error rates for Amazon S3 requests in the US-EAST-2 Region.
elevated API error rates and latencies in the US-WEST-1 Region
We are investigating increased API error rates to Lambda invocations from Amazon Lex in the US-EAST-1 Region.
Beginning at 5:15 AM we began experiencing increased latencies for Build operations.

We are experiencing increased API error rates for the EC2 APIs in the US-WEST-2 Region. Some APIs may experience errors or “request limit exceeded” when calling an affected API or using the EC2 Management Console. During this time, customers may have experienced 500 Errors or "Request Limit Exceeded" when calling an affected API, or using the EC2 Management Console.

We are investigating increased API error rates and latencies in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region. Between 3:46 PM and 5:08 PM PDT we experienced slightly increased latencies for the EC2 APIs in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.

We are investigating authentication issues affecting workspace availability in the US-WEST-2 Region. We have identified the cause of the authentication issue affecting some workspaces in the US-WEST-2 Region. Between October 4 11:00 PM and October 5 1:07 AM PDT, we experienced authentication issue affecting some workspaces in the US-WEST-2 Region.
customers may have experienced elevated latency and errors when accessing AWS APIs and consoles in the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region.
We are investigating increased error rates impacting start up of clusters operations in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region

We are investigating increased API error rates for the SubscribeToShard API in the US-WEST-2 Region. Between 06:20 AM and 09:56 AM PDT we experienced an increased error rate for the SubscribeToShard API, affecting some customers in the US-WEST-2 Region.

We are investigating increased API error rates in the eu-central-1 Region. We have identified the root cause of the issue causing increased elevated error rates in the eu-central-1 Region. We are starting to see some recovery in API errors in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Regions. Between 1:10 PM PDT and 5:26 PM PDT we experienced increased error rates in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region.
We are experiencing increased latencies while provisioning new ElastiCache nodes and elevated API error rates in the EU-WEST-1 Region.

We are investigating increased API error rates when establishing new connections to AWS IoT Core in the EU-WEST-1 Region. Between 1:52 AM and 3:35 AM PDT we experienced increased API error rates and latencies in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
Increased invoke timeouts and elevated latencies for AWS Lambda functions in the EU-WEST-1 Region. The issue affects a subset of functions making API calls to other AWS services within the region.
We are investigating increased error rates for API invokes in the EU-WEST-1 Region.

We are investigating delayed ENI attachment times for EC2 instances within a single Availability Zone (USE1-AZ4) in the US-EAST-1 Region. Newly launched EC2 instances or new ENI attachments, may experience delay in establishing network connectivity within the affected Availability Zone.

We are investigating network connectivity issues for some instances in a single Availability Zone in the EU-WEST-1 Region. Network connectivity has been restored for the vast majority of the affected instances in a single Availability Zone in the EU-WEST-1 Region. Some EBS volumes within the affected Availability Zone are also experiencing degraded performance. Starting at 5:35 AM PDT we experienced power and network connectivity issues for some instances, and degraded performance for some EBS volumes in a single Availability Zone in the EU-WEST-1 Region.

We are investigating network connectivity issues for some instances and increased error rates and latencies for the EC2 APIs within the US-EAST-2 Region. Some functions are experiencing invocation failures and latencies. EC2 API error rates and latencies have returned to normal levels.
customers experienced elevated DNS failures when querying Route 53 Resolver for certain hostnames in a single Availability Zone (use1-az4) in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We are currently investigating degraded performance for some EBS volumes in a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We are investigating connectivity issues affecting some database instances in a single Availability Zone in the EU-WEST-2 Region.

We are investigating an issue with an external provider outside of our network, which may be impacting Internet connectivity between some customer networks and the SA-EAST-1 Region. Connectivity to instances and services within the Region is not impacted by the event. Between 10:34 AM and 11:41 AM PDT we experienced elevated packet loss to and from some destinations on the Internet in the SA-EAST-1 Region.

We are investigating network connectivity issues for some instances in a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-1 Region. We can confirm that some instances are unreachable and some EBS volumes are experiencing degraded performance in a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-1 Region.

We are investigating increased error rates for (some) new launches in a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-1 Region. Between July 21 11:45 PM and July 22 12:56 AM PDT we experienced increased error rates for instance launches in the US-EAST-1 Region. Existing instances were unaffected.

We are investigating connectivity issues for some EC2 instances in a single Availability Zone (euc1-az1) in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region. We can confirm increased API error rates and latencies for the EC2 APIs and connectivity issues for instances within a single Availability Zone (euc1-az1) within the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region. Some EBS volumes are also experiencing degraded performance within the affected Availability Zone.

some instances experienced connectivity issues within a single Availability Zone in the EU-WEST-2 Region. The affected instances did not lose power but became unreachable due to a loss of power to networking devices within the affected Availability Zone. Some EBS volumes experienced degraded performance during this time period and launches of new EC2 instances and the creation of new EBS volumes experienced increased error rates.

Storage Gateway VMs appear to be offline and are not able to perform out of cache reads or upload to our service. Gateways were unable to perform out of cache reads and appeared offline in the SGW console. Gateways continued to accept writes but were unable to upload them to the AWS service.
Between 5:31 AM and 6:42 AM PDT we experienced increased error rates for instance launches in a single availability zone in the US-EAST-1 Region. Existing instances were unaffected.
Between 12:30 AM and 7:10 AM PST we experience periods of network connectivity issues for NAT Gateway within the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.
We can confirm Internet and EC2 Public IP address connectivity issues for newly launched EC2 instances within the US-GOV-EAST-1 Region. Connectivity for existing instances is not affected.

We are investigating connectivity issues affecting some instances in a single Availability Zone in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region. Some instances are impaired and some EBS volumes are experiencing degraded performance. Some EC2 APIs are also experiencing increased error rates and latencies.
customers experienced elevated network packet loss that impacted connectivity to a subset of Internet destinations.
We are investigating network connectivity issues affecting Direct Connect customers using the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region.
some database instances in the AP-SOUTH-1 Region are experiencing degraded performance or are unavailable.
a small number of instances experienced connectivity issues in a single Availability Zone in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.
some customers experienced connectivity issues from the East Coast of the United States to AWS services.

We are investigating increased API and launch error rates in a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-1 Region. Between 5:06 AM and 5:50 AM PST we experienced elevated error rates for instance related APIs and new instance launches in a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-1 Region.

We are investigating increased error rates for new launches in the EU-WEST-1 Region. We can confirm increased API error rates and connectivity issues for some instances in a single Availability Zone in the EU-WEST-1 Region. We continue to investigate connectivity issues from some instances to some AWS services in the EU-WEST-1 Region. We have resolved the connectivity issues from EC2 instances to the affected AWS services in the EU-WEST-1 Region. We continue to see elevated error rates for the RunInstances EC2 API, which we are working to resolve.
We are investigating meeting availability issues in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We are investigating connectivity issues affecting some instances in a single Availability Zone in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region.
Internet connectivity issue with an external provider outside of our network which affected traffic from some end-user networks and the AP-SOUTH-1 Region.

Storage Gateway VMs appear to be offline and are not able to perform out of cache reads or upload to our service. Gateways were unable to perform out of cache reads and appeared offline in the SGW console. Gateways continued to accept writes but were unable to upload them to the AWS service.

We are investigating increased failures for newly launched instances within the US-EAST-2 Region. Changes to instance networking state, such as mapping Elastic IP addresses, may also experience increased error rates. Some APIs, such as those used to attach Elastic IP addresses to instances, were also affected by this event. Some AWS services, such as Elastic Load Balancing and Elastic Map Reduce, experienced provisioning delays due to failures in launching new EC2 instances.

We are investigating increased EC2 launch failures and networking connectivity issues for some instances in a single Availability Zone (USE1-AZ4) in the US-EAST-1 Region. Customers experiencing connectivity or instance availability issues within the affected Availability Zone, should start to see some recovery as power is restored to the affected data center. RunInstances API error rates are returning to normal levels and we are working to recover affected EC2 instances and EBS volumes. For the remaining EC2 instances, we are experiencing some network connectivity issues, which is slowing down full recovery. We have a small number of affected EBS volumes that are still experiencing degraded IO performance that we are working to recover.
customers experienced Internet connectivity issues for some networks to and from the US-EAST-2 Region. Some customers may be experiencing VPN connectivity due to this issue.
customers experienced increased DNS query timeouts in a single Availability Zone (use1-az6) of the US-EAST-1 Region.

We are investigating longer than usual propagation times to propagate certain configuration changes made either via console, CloudFront APIs, or AWS CloudFormation templates to some of our edge locations. End-user requests for content from our edge locations are not affected by this issue and are being served normally.

We are investigating increased propagation times of DNS edits to public and private Route 53 hosted zones, and increased failures for Delete Hosted Zone, Associate Private Hosted Zone and Disassociate Private Hosted Zone API and Console operations. DNS updates are being accepted by the Route 53 API and Console, however an error is preventing the updates from being propagated from the Route53 API system to the live DNS servers. Until we have fully recovered, customers may receive an error for the following APIs: CreateHostedZone, DeleteHostedZone, AssociateVPCWithHostedZone, and DisassociateVPCFromHostedZone.
Customers may have experienced longer than usual propagation times while making changes to CloudFront configurations.

We are investigating increased propagation times of DNS edits to Route 53 DNS servers. Queries to existing DNS records are not affected by this issue and are being answered normally. This issue will also affect provisioning of services such as EFS, ElasticBeanstalk, CloudFormation, and Amazon MQ.

We are investigating delays in propagating changes to CloudFront distributions to our edge locations. CloudFront distribution changes continue to be affected by the AWS Certificate Manager issue in US-EAST-1. We experienced delays in propagating changes to CloudFront distributions to our edge locations.

We are investigating elevated error rates and elevated latency in multiple edge locations. We can confirm elevated error rates and high latency accessing content from multiple Edge Locations, which is also contributing to longer than usual propagation times for changes to CloudFront configurations. Starting 3:18 PM PDT, we experienced elevated error rates and high latency accessing content from multiple Edge Locations. During this time, customers may also have experienced longer than usual change propagation delays for CloudFront configurations and invalidations.
We are currently experiencing elevated error rates for the Route 53 Management Console.
We are investigating DNS resolution issues from some instances in the EU-WEST-1 Region.

We’re investigating longer than usual propagation times for changes to CloudFront configurations. End-user requests for content from our edge locations are not affected by this issue and are being served normally.

We are investigating longer than usual reporting update delays for change propagation of invalidations and CloudFront configurations. CloudFront Access Logs, Metrics and Reporting may also be affected. CloudFront Access Logs, Metrics, and Reporting continues to be affected by the Kinesis event. Between 5:41 AM and 11:26 PM PST, CloudFront Real-time Metrics were not available.
This is causing DNS lookups through these resolvers for a small number of AWS names to fail.

We are investigating longer than usual propagation times for changes to CloudFront configurations. End-user requests for content from our edge locations are not affected by this issue and are being served normally.

We are investigating increased propagation times of DNS edits to public and private Route 53 hosted zones, and increased failures for Delete Hosted Zone, Associate Private Hosted Zone and Disassociate Private Hosted Zone API and Console operations. DNS updates are being accepted by the Route 53 API and Console, however an error is preventing the updates from being propagated from the Route53 API system to the live DNS servers. Until we have fully recovered, customers may receive an error for the following APIs: CreateHostedZone, DeleteHostedZone, AssociateVPCWithHostedZone, and DisassociateVPCFromHostedZone.
increased delays in validation and issuance for DNS validated domains in the US-EAST-1 Region. Certificates can be requested but will be delayed in issuance.
elevated SSL handshake failures and BadRequest errors from CloudFront edge locations
Customers experienced delays propagating changes submitted to the Route 53 API, as well as increased API error rates. This also affected provisioning of new resources that rely on Route 53 DNS.
We are investigating increased propagation times of DNS edits to the Route 53 DNS servers. This does not impact queries to existing DNS records.

We are investigating reports that domain nameservers may unintentionally change for domains registered under .ch, .com.au, .co.nz, .es, .im, .it, .jp, .net.au, .nl, .nz, .se and .sh, which are registered through our third party registrar partner.
We are investigating increased propagation times of DNS edits to the Route 53 DNS servers. This does not impact queries to existing DNS records.
some customers in Japan had a small percentage of DNS lookups fail when resolving CloudFront distributions
Customers may have experienced longer than usual propagation times while making changes to CloudFront configurations.

We are investigating increased errors when making changes to some hosted zones, and creation of new hosted zones using the Route 53 API. DNS queries are not affected by this issue, which are all being answered as normal.

Customers might have experienced delays in reporting status change of CloudFront invalidations. During this time, all CloudFront edge locations were consuming configuration changes and invalidations normally but were not updating status changes in console or via CloudFront APIs.
End-user requests for content from our edge locations are not affected by this issue and are being served normally.

We are currently investigating intermittent errors connecting to some custom non-S3 Origins from our Regional Edge Caches. We are investigating an intermittent issue resulting in errors resolving origin domains hosted at an external provider outside of the AWS network. Between 1:00 PM and 3:35 PM PST some Non-S3 CloudFront origins domains hosted by a single external DNS provider experienced intermittent resolution errors.

Customers experienced issues resolving newly created public EC2 instance names to private IPs within newly created VPCs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Creation of new Route 53 Resolver Endpoints was also delayed during this time.
We are investigating increased propagation times of DNS edits to the Route 53 DNS servers. This does not impact queries to existing DNS records.
We are investigating slow propagation of DNS edits to the Route 53 DNS servers. Requests to the ChangeResourceRecordSets API are currently being throttled.
We are investigating increased propagation times of DNS edits to Route 53 DNS servers. Queries to existing DNS records are not affected by this issue.
